data.title: New Wallet Receives Significant Bitcoin Transfer
data.description: According to TechFlow, on November 20, blockchain analysis platform Lookonchain reported that a newly created wallet, identified as '3AXFhi,' received 1,300 BTC, valued at $120.6 million, from BitGo within the past six hours.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $37.3518 million yesterday, marking the seventh consecutive day of net outflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $37.3518 million yesterday (November 19th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the Invesco ETF QETH, with a net inflow of $2.9322 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $22.4123 million. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the BlackRock ETF ETHA, with a net outflow of $24.5899 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $13.066 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs was $18.194 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 5.12%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $12.838 billion.

data.title: Cloudflare outage shows why crypto needs end-to-end decentralization
data.description:
Many blockchains are decentralized, but their frontends and storage still rely on Web2 — a weakness exposed when the Cloudflare outage knocked several platforms offline, EthStorage said.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/crypto-needs-end-to-end-decentralization?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss%3Fvfff%3D1763613795%26_ts%3D1763613795115&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $75.4696 million yesterday, marking the first net inflow after five days of net outflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $75.4696 million yesterday (November 19th, Eastern Time). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was BlackRock ETF IBIT, with a net inflow of $60.6084 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $63.181 billion. This was followed by Grayscale Bitcoin Mini Trust ETF BTC, with a net inflow of $53.8383 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $1.87 billion. The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was Fidelity ETF FBTC, with a net outflow of $21.3517 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $11.881 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $117.345 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) is 6.57%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $58.3 billion.

data.title: Gate Alpha launches its 112th round of points airdrops; holders of the corresponding points can be among the first to claim 0.6 or 2.8 GT.
data.description: According to official news, Gate Alpha will launch its 112th GT points airdrop on November 20th at 18:00 (UTC+8). This airdrop will use a tiered airdrop model with varying amounts of points. Users holding 129 to 168 Gate Alpha points can receive 0.6 GT airdrop tokens, consuming 12 Gate Alpha points; those holding 169 or more Gate Alpha points can receive 2.8 GT airdrop tokens, consuming 15 Gate Alpha points. Gate Alpha currently supports popular public chains such as SOL, ETH, Gate Layer, BNB Chain, Base, SUI, ARB, World Chain, AVAX, Polygon, LINEA, ZK, and OP. It also enables seamless trading of tokens across the entire chain through contract address search, facilitating cross-chain transactions and allowing users to access all on-chain tokens with a single click.

data.title: Quantum Computing Poses Potential Threat to Cryptography by 2028, Says Vitalik Buterin
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Vitalik Buterin recently warned at the Devconnect conference that quantum computing could potentially break elliptic curve cryptography by the 2028 U.S. presidential election. This statement has sparked intense debate within the community.

Crypto influencer Charles Edwards noted that mainstream experts are already assessing the risks of quantum computing impacting Bitcoin, aligning with Vitalik's three-year prediction. He emphasized that the crypto industry can no longer ignore this risk. In contrast, physics expert David M. Antonelli argued against this view, stating that even the most optimistic forecasts from companies like IBM, Google, and Quantinuum predict only a few thousand physical quantum bits by 2030, rather than millions of logical quantum bits.

Additionally, Tom Lee shared reports related to Vitalik's warning about the threat of quantum computing to cryptographic algorithms.

While there is debate over whether quantum computing will pose a threat to the crypto industry by 2028, the community generally agrees that based on the current pace of research, quantum computing will indeed threaten cryptographic algorithms in the foreseeable future.

data.title: The author of "Rich Dad Poor Dad" refutes claim: He never said gold would plummet by 50% in December.
data.description: Robert Kiyosaki, author of "Rich Dad Poor Dad," posted a statement on the X platform to refute rumors, pointing out that some media platforms had released fake videos in which a highly convincing impersonator claimed that gold would plummet by 50% in December. Robert Kiyosaki clarified that he has never made such a statement and reminded the community to be wary of misinformation. He has previously stated on multiple occasions that he holds gold, silver, Bitcoin, and Ethereum because he distrusts the Federal Reserve, the U.S. Treasury, and the Wall Street system, and refers to gold and silver as "God's money," while Bitcoin and Ethereum are "the people's money."

data.title: Morph announced an update to the BGB token economy, launching a new quarterly burning mechanism and expanding it to DeFi and PayFi applications.
data.description: The Morph Foundation today announced its latest allocation plan for its 220 million BGB tokens and officially launched a new quarterly BGB burn mechanism. This mechanism directly links the supply of BGB to the actual usage of the Morph network, with the burn scale determined by ecosystem fees, average price, and community governance parameters. With the Viridian upgrade and support for EIP-7702, BGB can be directly used to pay Morph network gas, becoming one of the first ERC-20 tokens to function as native gas on Layer 2. Morph stated that this update not only strengthens BGB's core role in gas, governance, and settlement but will also drive its further expansion in key ecosystem areas such as DeFi and PayFi, creating a long-term deflationary path for the token based on real-world use cases.

data.title: BOB will undergo TGE today, with an initial airdrop of 4.15% of the total supply.
data.description: Bitcoin DeFi project BOB announced on the X platform that it will conduct a TGE (Transfer-to-Given) event today. The initial airdrop will account for 4.15% of the total supply, with 2.15% allocated to eligible addresses that have participated in the BOB ecosystem and other activities, and the remaining 2% as staking rewards. This requires staking BOB tokens for 3, 6, or 18 months, with longer staking periods yielding higher rewards.
